name: Azure AI Document Intelligence API
what_it_does: Microsoft Azure AI Document Intelligence (formerly Azure Form Recognizer) REST API for intelligent document processing, OCR, and structured data extraction from documents and forms. Enables AI agents to manage document OCR and text extraction for document digitization automation, handle prebuilt model extraction for invoices, receipts, IDs, tax forms (W-2, 1099), contracts, and business cards, access custom model training for domain-specific document types, retrieve layout analysis with table, paragraph, and structure extraction for complex document understanding, manage batch document analysis for high-volume processing workflows, handle key-value pair extraction for general form processing, access multi-language OCR across 300+ languages for global document processing, retrieve confidence scoring and bounding polygon data for extraction quality validation, manage document classification for automated routing, and integrate Document Intelligence with Azure Blob Storage, Cognitive Search, and Power Automate.
use_cases: Extracting invoice data from AP automation agents
Processing IDs from KYC verification agents
Parsing tax forms from financial document agents
Classifying documents from Azure workflow routing agents
not_for: Real-time document processing requiring sub-500ms response
Complex document workflow orchestration without Azure ecosystem
best_when: An Azure-native organization wanting AI agents to automate document OCR, invoice extraction, ID verification, tax form processing, and structured data extraction using Microsoft's prebuilt and custom document models with Azure ecosystem integration.
avoid_when: MODEL VERSION PINNING MANDATORY: Azure AI Document Intelligence model versions (2023-07-31, 2024-02-29, 2024-11-30) have different output field names and schema; automated agents must pin to a specific GA model version; automated reliance on 'latest' model causes breaking schema changes at Microsoft's release cadence. GDPR EU DATA RESIDENCY FOR PERSONAL DATA: Automated Document Intelligence processing of EU personal data documents must use EU Azure regions (West Europe, North Europe, France Central); automated routing to US Azure region for EU PII documents creates GDPR Chapter V data transfer violation. HIPAA PHI MINIMUM NECESSARY FOR HEALTHCARE: Automated healthcare document processing with Document Intelligence requires Azure HIPAA BAA; automated extraction and storage of all PHI fields beyond specific workflow purpose creates HIPAA minimum necessary violation. ENTRA ID SERVICE PRINCIPAL FOR AGENTS: Automated agent authentication must use Entra ID service principal with Cognitive Services User role; Entra ID conditional access policies can block service principal token acquisition without managed identity exemption.

auth.notes: Azure AI Document Intelligence uses Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Redmond, Washington HQ (Microsoft Corporation, NASDAQ: MSFT). $220B+ cloud run-rate. Azure AI Document Intelligence rebranded from Azure Form Recognizer 2023. Prebuilt models for 15+ document types. GA model versions: 2023-07-31, 2024-02-29, 2024-11-30. Competes with Google Document AI and AWS Textract for cloud document AI.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 500 pages/month free for most features; custom model training pages billed separately
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-page pricing by feature (layout, prebuilt, custom); custom model training additional
pricing.notes: Redmond WA. Microsoft NASDAQ:MSFT. Azure consumption billing. 500 free pages/month. Prebuilt + custom model pricing. Custom model training billed per page.

performance.rate_limits: Transactions per second per endpoint; quota limits per Azure subscription tier; batch async for high volume

known_agent_gotchas: MODEL VERSION PINNING FOR SCHEMA STABILITY: Azure Document Intelligence model API versions (2023-07-31, 2024-02-29, 2024-11-30) have materially different output field schemas; always pin to specific GA model version in API URL; automated use of 'latest' alias causes downstream schema breakage when Microsoft releases new model version

ASYNC OPERATION REQUIRED FOR LARGE DOCUMENTS: Documents >2MB or multi-page documents must use async analyze operation (beginAnalyzeDocument) with operation-id polling; automated synchronous calls for large documents fail with 400 error; implement polling with exponential backoff for operation completion

ENTRA ID SERVICE PRINCIPAL CONDITIONAL ACCESS: Automated agent service principals require Cognitive Services User or Contributor role; Entra ID conditional access policies (MFA, compliant device) can block service principal token acquisition; use managed identity for compute-based agents to bypass conditional access

EU GDPR DATA RESIDENCY REGION SELECTION: Document Intelligence endpoint must be in same Azure region as data residency requirement; EU PII documents must use West Europe, North Europe, or France Central endpoints; cross-region processing creates GDPR Chapter V transfer risk

Custom model training data labeling quality — custom Document Intelligence model accuracy is bounded by training data label quality; automated training on documents with inconsistent field labeling produces low-accuracy extraction; require human label review before automated model training submission

Output field name changes between model versions — prebuilt invoice model field names changed between 2023-07-31 and 2024-02-29 API versions (e.g., VendorName→MerchantName for receipts); automated parsing code must be version-specific; test downstream parsing on each model version upgrade
agent_readiness.security_notes: Azure cloud document AI. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP. Entra ID OAuth2/API key. Global Azure regions. Document content and extracted PII.

security_score_components.security_notes: Azure AI document intelligence. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP. Entra ID OAuth2. Global Azure regions. Document content and personal data.
